intended_use: The qXR-BT device is intended to generate a secondary digital chest X-ray image that facilitates confirmation of the position of a breathing tube and an anatomical landmark on adult chest X-rays. This
intended_use_full: The qXR-BT device is intended to generate a secondary digital chest X-ray image that facilitates confirmation of the position of a breathing tube and an anatomical landmark on adult chest X-rays. This device is intended for use by licensed physicians who are trained in the evaluation of breathing tube placement on chest X-rays. The qXR-BT image provides adjunctive information and is not a substitute for the original PA/AP image.
target_condition: Confirmation of breathing tube (tracheal tube) position on chest X-rays
ground_truth: Manual annotation by three radiologists from United States
other_metrics: Carina localization absolute distance: Mean 2.15 mm (SD 1.25), Median 1.86 mm (10th-90th percentile 0.63-4.05 mm), Mean 95% CI 2.15 (1.96-2.35) mm; Breathing tube tip absolute distance: Mean 1.97 mm (SD 1.09), Median 1.80 mm (10th-90th percentile 0.7-3.64 mm), Mean 95% CI 1.97 (1.80-2.13) mm; Distance between tip and carina absolute error: Mean 1.98 mm (SD 1.41), Median 1.64 mm (10th-90th percentile 0.27-4.12 mm), Mean 95% CI 1.98 (1.76-2.20) mm
